title: Industrial metals extend gains as China announces further stimulus measures
section_path: Industrial metals extend gains as China announces further stimulus measures
section_path_text: Industrial metals extend gains as China announces further stimulus measures
summary:
Main
Further stimulus measures in China helped push industrial metals higher. Oil markets gave up earlier gains as supply disruptions eased. By Daniel Hynes
Image reference: Στιγμιότυπο οθόνης 2024-09-26 081836.png | assets/2024-09-26_industrial-metals-extend-gains-as-china-announces-further-stimulus-measures_img_cea3cf84ceb9ceb3cebcceb9cf8ccf84cf85_43c949a5d3f1.png

Market Commentary
Iron ore extended its rally on hopes China's huge stimulus package would elicit more demand.

children.title: Main
children.summary: Further stimulus measures in China helped push industrial metals higher. Oil markets gave up earlier gains as supply disruptions eased.

children.title: Market Commentary
children.summary: Iron ore extended its rally on hopes China's huge stimulus package would elicit more demand. The steel making raw material threatened to break above USD100/t for the first time in a month after Beijing unveiled a broad suite of measures to support its economy.

children.title: Chart of the Day
children.summary:
Another weekly drawdown in US oil inventories signals strong demand in the world's biggest consumer. Image reference: Στιγμιότυπο οθόνης 2024-09-26 081929.png | assets/2024-09-26_industrial-metals-extend-gains-as-china-announces-further-stimulus-measures_img_cea3cf84ceb9ceb3cebcceb9cf8ccf84cf85_12365cec35b4.png
